key_prs.author: eric-ships

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Motivation

B20 precompiles had no end-to-end coverage exercising the full RPC path through a live devnet node. This adds 34 tests across four files covering the complete B20 and PolicyRegistry feature surface.

## What changed

- \`activation_registry.rs\` (3 tests): isActivated default, admin getter, unauthorized activate revert
- \`b20_precompile.rs\` (10 tests): factory create and transfer, metadata, approve/transferFrom, mint/burn, transferWithMemo, supply cap, metadata updates, pause/unpause, address prediction and isB20, duplicate salt revert
- \`policy_registry.rs\` (1 test): sentinel policyExists(0) = true
- \`policy_transfer.rs\` (8 tests): allowlist/blocklist gating, always-block, non-admin mutation reverts, batch membership atomicity, authorization persistence across blocks, default ALWAYS\_ALLOW before policy is wired
- \`devnet/src/b20.rs\`: \`B20PrecompileClient\` and \`B20CreateConfig\` RPC helpers shared across all B20 devnet tests

type=standard
risk=low
impact=sev5
commit_subjects: chore(BASE-000): update main branch to include some other changes
test(policy): add 5 unit tests to PolicyRegistry storage
feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates
feat(precompiles): add base-common-precompiles crate and Beryl action tests
test(devnet): add B20 and PolicyRegistry E2E tests
fix(lychee): exclude pending-merge file links from link checker

key_prs.title: test(devnet): add B20 and PolicyRegistry E2E tests

key_prs.author: eric-ships

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Motivation

B20 tokens are implemented entirely as stateful native precompiles. This introduces the implementation crate and the action test harness that validates each precompile function across the Beryl fork boundary.

## What changed

**base-common-precompiles**: implements all B20 precompiles using \`base-precompile-storage\` for slot layout. Includes B20 factory, base token interface (ERC-20, roles, pause, permit, supply cap, memo, metadata), stablecoin and security token variants, PolicyRegistry (allowlist/blocklist with two-step admin transfer and built-in sentinel policies), and ActivationRegistry for feature gating.

**Beryl action tests** (\`actions/harness/tests/beryl/\`): 60+ \`#[tokio::test]\` functions validating the full precompile ABI surface across the Beryl activation boundary. Covers factory creation and deterministic addressing, stablecoin and security ERC-20 lifecycle, supply cap, pause/unpause, role grant/revoke, EIP-2612 permit, memo transfers, security share ratio and batch ops, policy gating (allowlist/blocklist/always-block, combined sender+receiver, mid-lifecycle update), and activation registry feature lifecycle.

**PolicyRegistry unit tests**: 6 ...
commit_subjects: chore(BASE-000): update main branch to include some other changes
test(policy): add 5 unit tests to PolicyRegistry storage
feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates
feat(precompiles): add base-common-precompiles crate and Beryl action tests
fix(lychee): exclude pending-merge file links from link checker

key_prs.title: feat(precompiles): add base-common-precompiles crate and Beryl action tests

key_prs.author: eric-ships

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Motivation

B20 precompiles need a typed, ERC-7201-namespaced storage layer that compiles to deterministic slot layouts without manual slot arithmetic. This introduces the two crates that form that foundation.

## What changed

**base-precompile-macros**: proc-macro crate providing \`#[contract]\`, \`#[namespace]\`, and \`#[derive(Storable)]\` attribute macros. Generates ERC-7201 namespace roots at compile time and enforces mutual exclusion between slot attributes.

**base-precompile-storage**: typed storage layer with \`Vec<T>\`, \`[T; N]\`, \`Mapping<K,V>\`, \`Set<T>\`, \`Slot<T>\`, \`Bytes\`/\`String\`, and packed primitives. EVM provider implements EIP-8037 state gas accounting for new account code deposits.

**base-alloy-chains**: adds \`Azul\` and \`Beryl\` hardfork variants required by the precompile spec layer.

Unit test additions per type: Mapping CRUD and key isolation, Set duplicate/remove idempotency and U256 elements, primitives U256 boundaries and u8 packing, Slot overwrite and delete, Bytes short overwrite and empty roundtrip, EIP-8037 gas scaling with code length, exhaustion revert, two-account sum, namespace case sensitivity produces distinct roots.

type=st...

test(policy): add 5 unit tests to PolicyRegistry storage
feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates
fix(lychee): exclude pending-merge file links from link checker

key_prs.title: feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This fixes the SP1 aggregation ELF build by enabling Alloy serde support where the aggregation program CBOR-decodes L1 headers. It also aligns the nested SP1 programs workspace with the root Alloy consensus version so host and guest header serialization use the same major version.
commit_subjects: fix(proof): enable sp1 aggregation header serde

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This adds Beryl action-harness coverage for the B-20 security token variant. The tests cover factory creation, dynamic ABI readbacks, security-specific mutations and events, invalid input reverts, and activation gating across derivation. The coverage uses the returndata hash staticcall probe so string and array payloads are asserted directly.
commit_subjects: test(action-harness): add B20 security action tests
test(action-harness): extract beryl staticcall helpers
Merge branch 'main' into rf/test/add-b20-security-action-tests
fix(action-harness): repair beryl helper compile errors

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This removes the checked-in Claude skill artifact from the repo and keeps future local Claude artifacts out of Git. The change is limited to deleting the local skill file and adding .claude to .gitignore.
commit_subjects: chore(tooling): remove claude skills

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This removes the custom test-only batch mint and batch burn helpers from the B20 security dispatch tests. The coverage now enters through encoded IB20Security batchMint and batchBurn calls, so it exercises selector decoding, activation, caller roles, and real event and state behavior.
commit_subjects: test(b20-security): exercise batch mint burn dispatch

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This strengthens the Beryl action-test staticcall probe so it records both the returned byte length and the keccak hash of full returndata while preserving the existing first-word checks. The B20 staticcall cases now assert exact ABI payloads for dynamic returns such as token metadata, paused features, contract URI, and EIP-712 domain output.
commit_subjects: test(action-harness): assert Beryl staticcall returndata
fix(ci): avoid setup-just in zepter workflow
Merge branch 'main' into rf/test/assert-beryl-returndata
fix(action-harness): align stablecoin probe assertions

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This stabilizes devnet test runs by serializing shared Docker setup paths and aligning tests with the current activation and role requirements. The setup image build now uses a lock so parallel tests do not race on the local tag. The B20 and policy registry tests explicitly perform the role and feature setup required by the current precompile behavior.
commit_subjects: fix(devnet): stabilize devnet tests

key_prs.author: mw2000

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Add a clap-configured B-20 ZK dry-run proving benchmark that reports per-operation gas and cycle tracker output.
- Add the devnet bench entrypoint, Justfile helper, B-20 receipt reporting support, and Succinct ELF/Docker enforcement updates needed by the benchmark.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
commit_subjects: feat(devnet): add B20 zk proving benchmark

key_prs.title: feat(devnet): Add B20 ZK proving benchmark

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Documentation only follow up to #2914.

While reading the B20 token ops I noticed that `privileged: bool` has different semantics across the ops that take it, and only `Transferable::transfer` documents what `privileged` actually does. The other four traits (`Mintable`, `Burnable`, `Pausable`, `Configurable`) take the same parameter but only skip the role check under it pause and policy guards still apply.

This PR doesn't change any behavior. It just adds short doc comments on `mint`, `burn`, `pause`, `unpause`, `update_supply_cap`, `update_name`, `update_symbol`, and `update_contract_uri` calling out:
1. Which role check is skipped when `privileged = true`.
2. The fact that, unlike `Transferable::transfer`, no other guards are bypassed.
3. A reference to issue #2914 so the asymmetry can be tracked in one place.

If #2914 ends up being resolved by changing the behavior of one side, these docs should be updated to match. For now this just makes the current asymmetry discoverable from the trait definitions.

No test or code changes `git diff --stat`:
commit_subjects: docs(precompiles): clarify privileged semantics for mint/burn/pause/configurable ops

closed_unmerged.title: docs(precompiles): clarify `privileged` semantics for mint/burn/pause/configurable ops

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Spotted a broken GitHub line anchor in a doc comment. The link for `MIN_TX_SIZE_SCALED` uses `#82` instead of `#L82`, so it doesn't jump to line 82 on op-geth (just opens the file at the top). The two adjacent references on the same op-geth file use the correct `#L78` and `#L79` format.

One character fix, no behavior change.
commit_subjects: docs(flz): fix broken line anchor in op-geth reference link

closed_unmerged.title: docs(flz): fix broken line anchor in op geth reference link

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This adds a dedicated CI workflow that cross-validates the current Base checkout against base-std. The job clones base-std and base-anvil, builds patched anvil and forge binaries with the Base precompile crates patched to the current branch, then runs the base-std fork-test script. Forge test failures propagate through the script exit code, so parity regressions fail CI.
commit_subjects: chore(infra): add base-std fork test ci
fix(infra): authenticate base-anvil clone
fix(infra): use public base-anvil clone
chore(ci): retrigger after github auth failure
fix(infra): build base-anvil with its toolchain
fix(infra): speed up base-anvil fork-test build

merged.title: chore(infra): Add Base Std Fork Tests

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This updates the zkvm precompile cycle-tracker wrapper to use the current Alloy precompile mapping API after the Reth v2 dependency update. The fix preserves the tracked-precompile behavior while avoiding the removed map_pure_precompiles method that was blocking the SP1 ELF manifest refresh workflow.
commit_subjects: fix(proofs): update zkvm precompile mapping

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Add reusable display, provider, report, and ZK proof helpers for devnet benchmark targets.
- Add the dev-only proof and progress dependencies used by benchmark utilities.


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
commit_subjects: feat(devnet): add shared bench utilities

merged.title: feat(devnet): Add shared devnet bench utilities

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Third PR in the EIP-8130 rollout. Builds on #2863 (types) and #2866 (rename + match-arm reorder), both merged.

### Context

#2863 wired the EIP-8130 transaction type (`0x7D`) through consensus, envelope, receipt, and reth-compat plumbing so the type byte is recognised and round-trips through the typed-transaction layer.

However **no validation, mempool admission, signature verification, or execution path exists yet** \u2014 those land in later PRs of this rollout (pool stub, 2D-nonce pool, fork gate, precompiles, verifiers, execution core, etc.).

Until that work lands, any `0x7D` payload submitted via JSON-RPC must be rejected at the RPC boundary so it cannot leak into the mempool or be silently dropped after partial processing.

### Changes

**1. Explicit rejection in `eth_sendRawTransaction`** (two entry points)

- `base-execution-rpc` `EthTransactions::send_transaction`: after the pool transaction has been split out of `WithEncoded<Recovered<_>>`, compare its type byte against `EIP8130_TX_TYPE_ID` and return a new typed error variant `BaseInvalidTransactionError::Eip8130NotEnabled` (surfaces as JSON-RPC `TransactionRejected`).
- `ingress-rpc-lib` `IngressServic...
commit_subjects: feat(consensus): add BasePooledTransaction::is_eip8130 helper
feat(rpc): reject EIP-8130 transactions at eth_sendRawTransaction boundary
test(rpc-types): assert EIP-8130 transactions serialize cleanly in eth_getTransactionByHash shape
feat(txpool): reject EIP-8130 transactions at validator boundary
refactor(rpc): rename Eip8130NotEnabled to Eip8130NotAccepted
fix(ingress-rpc): use TransactionRejected error code for EIP-8130
refactor(consensus): centralise EIP-8130 RPC rejection message

merged.title: feat(rpc): reject EIP-8130 transactions at eth_sendRawTransaction boundary

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Third PR in the EIP-8130 rollout. Builds on #2863 (types) and #2866 (rename + match-arm reorder), both merged.

### Context

#2863 wired the EIP-8130 transaction type (`0x7D`) through consensus, envelope, receipt, and reth-compat plumbing so the type byte is recognised and round-trips through the typed-transaction layer.

However **no validation, mempool admission, signature verification, or execution path exists yet** \u2014 those land in later PRs of this rollout (pool stub, 2D-nonce pool, fork gate, precompiles, verifiers, execution core, etc.).

Until that work lands, any `0x7D` payload submitted via JSON-RPC must be rejected at the RPC boundary so it cannot leak into the mempool or be silently dropped after partial processing.

### Changes

**1. Explicit rejection in `eth_sendRawTransaction`** (two entry points)

- `base-execution-rpc` `EthTransactions::send_transaction`: after the pool transaction has been split out of `WithEncoded<Recovered<_>>`, compare its type byte against `EIP8130_TX_TYPE_ID` and return a new typed error variant `BaseInvalidTransactionError::Eip8130NotEnabled` (surfaces as JSON-RPC `TransactionRejected`).
- `ingress-rpc-lib` `IngressServic...

test(rpc-types): assert EIP-8130 transactions serialize cleanly in eth_getTransactionByHash shape
feat(txpool): reject EIP-8130 transactions at validator boundary

fix(ingress-rpc): use TransactionRejected error code for EIP-8130
refactor(consensus): centralise EIP-8130 RPC rejection message

merged.title: feat(rpc): reject EIP-8130 transactions at eth_sendRawTransaction boundary

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This adds a dedicated CI workflow that cross-validates the current Base checkout against base-std. The job clones base-std and base-anvil, builds patched anvil and forge binaries with the Base precompile crates patched to the current branch, then runs the base-std fork-test script. Forge test failures propagate through the script exit code, so parity regressions fail CI.
commit_subjects: chore(infra): add base-std fork test ci
fix(infra): authenticate base-anvil clone
fix(infra): use public base-anvil clone
chore(ci): retrigger after github auth failure
fix(infra): build base-anvil with its toolchain
fix(infra): speed up base-anvil fork-test build

merged.title: chore(infra): Add Base Std Fork Tests

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This fixes the SP1 aggregation ELF build by enabling Alloy serde support where the aggregation program CBOR-decodes L1 headers. It also aligns the nested SP1 programs workspace with the root Alloy consensus version so host and guest header serialization use the same major version.
commit_subjects: fix(proof): enable sp1 aggregation header serde

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This removes the checked-in Claude skill artifact from the repo and keeps future local Claude artifacts out of Git. The change is limited to deleting the local skill file and adding .claude to .gitignore.
commit_subjects: chore(tooling): remove claude skills

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This removes the custom test-only batch mint and batch burn helpers from the B20 security dispatch tests. The coverage now enters through encoded IB20Security batchMint and batchBurn calls, so it exercises selector decoding, activation, caller roles, and real event and state behavior.
commit_subjects: test(b20-security): exercise batch mint burn dispatch

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This updates the zkvm precompile cycle-tracker wrapper to use the current Alloy precompile mapping API after the Reth v2 dependency update. The fix preserves the tracked-precompile behavior while avoiding the removed map_pure_precompiles method that was blocking the SP1 ELF manifest refresh workflow.
commit_subjects: fix(proofs): update zkvm precompile mapping

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This stabilizes devnet test runs by serializing shared Docker setup paths and aligning tests with the current activation and role requirements. The setup image build now uses a lock so parallel tests do not race on the local tag. The B20 and policy registry tests explicitly perform the role and feature setup required by the current precompile behavior.
commit_subjects: fix(devnet): stabilize devnet tests

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This strengthens the Beryl action-test staticcall probe so it records both the returned byte length and the keccak hash of full returndata while preserving the existing first-word checks. The B20 staticcall cases now assert exact ABI payloads for dynamic returns such as token metadata, paused features, contract URI, and EIP-712 domain output.
commit_subjects: test(action-harness): assert Beryl staticcall returndata
fix(ci): avoid setup-just in zepter workflow
Merge branch 'main' into rf/test/assert-beryl-returndata
fix(action-harness): align stablecoin probe assertions

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Documentation only follow up to #2914.

While reading the B20 token ops I noticed that `privileged: bool` has different semantics across the ops that take it, and only `Transferable::transfer` documents what `privileged` actually does. The other four traits (`Mintable`, `Burnable`, `Pausable`, `Configurable`) take the same parameter but only skip the role check under it pause and policy guards still apply.

This PR doesn't change any behavior. It just adds short doc comments on `mint`, `burn`, `pause`, `unpause`, `update_supply_cap`, `update_name`, `update_symbol`, and `update_contract_uri` calling out:
1. Which role check is skipped when `privileged = true`.
2. The fact that, unlike `Transferable::transfer`, no other guards are bypassed.
3. A reference to issue #2914 so the asymmetry can be tracked in one place.

If #2914 ends up being resolved by changing the behavior of one side, these docs should be updated to match. For now this just makes the current asymmetry discoverable from the trait definitions.

No test or code changes `git diff --stat`:
commit_subjects: docs(precompiles): clarify privileged semantics for mint/burn/pause/configurable ops

opened.title: docs(precompiles): clarify `privileged` semantics for mint/burn/pause/configurable ops

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Spotted a broken GitHub line anchor in a doc comment. The link for `MIN_TX_SIZE_SCALED` uses `#82` instead of `#L82`, so it doesn't jump to line 82 on op-geth (just opens the file at the top). The two adjacent references on the same op-geth file use the correct `#L78` and `#L79` format.

One character fix, no behavior change.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Motivation

B20 precompiles need a typed, ERC-7201-namespaced storage layer that compiles to deterministic slot layouts without manual slot arithmetic. This introduces the two crates that form that foundation.

## What changed

**base-precompile-macros**: proc-macro crate providing \`#[contract]\`, \`#[namespace]\`, and \`#[derive(Storable)]\` attribute macros. Generates ERC-7201 namespace roots at compile time and enforces mutual exclusion between slot attributes.

**base-precompile-storage**: typed storage layer with \`Vec<T>\`, \`[T; N]\`, \`Mapping<K,V>\`, \`Set<T>\`, \`Slot<T>\`, \`Bytes\`/\`String\`, and packed primitives. EVM provider implements EIP-8037 state gas accounting for new account code deposits.

**base-alloy-chains**: adds \`Azul\` and \`Beryl\` hardfork variants required by the precompile spec layer.

Unit test additions per type: Mapping CRUD and key isolation, Set duplicate/remove idempotency and U256 elements, primitives U256 boundaries and u8 packing, Slot overwrite and delete, Bytes short overwrite and empty roundtrip, EIP-8037 gas scaling with code length, exhaustion revert, two-account sum, namespace case sensitivity produces distinct roots.

type=st...
commit_subjects: chore(BASE-000): update main branch to include some other changes
test(policy): add 5 unit tests to PolicyRegistry storage
feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates
fix(lychee): exclude pending-merge file links from link checker

opened.title: feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Motivation

B20 tokens are implemented entirely as stateful native precompiles. This introduces the implementation crate and the action test harness that validates each precompile function across the Beryl fork boundary.

## What changed

**base-common-precompiles**: implements all B20 precompiles using \`base-precompile-storage\` for slot layout. Includes B20 factory, base token interface (ERC-20, roles, pause, permit, supply cap, memo, metadata), stablecoin and security token variants, PolicyRegistry (allowlist/blocklist with two-step admin transfer and built-in sentinel policies), and ActivationRegistry for feature gating.

**Beryl action tests** (\`actions/harness/tests/beryl/\`): 60+ \`#[tokio::test]\` functions validating the full precompile ABI surface across the Beryl activation boundary. Covers factory creation and deterministic addressing, stablecoin and security ERC-20 lifecycle, supply cap, pause/unpause, role grant/revoke, EIP-2612 permit, memo transfers, security share ratio and batch ops, policy gating (allowlist/blocklist/always-block, combined sender+receiver, mid-lifecycle update), and activation registry feature lifecycle.

**PolicyRegistry unit tests**: 6 ...
commit_subjects: chore(BASE-000): update main branch to include some other changes
test(policy): add 5 unit tests to PolicyRegistry storage
feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates
feat(precompiles): add base-common-precompiles crate and Beryl action tests
fix(lychee): exclude pending-merge file links from link checker

opened.title: feat(precompiles): add base-common-precompiles crate and Beryl action tests

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Motivation

B20 precompiles had no end-to-end coverage exercising the full RPC path through a live devnet node. This adds 34 tests across four files covering the complete B20 and PolicyRegistry feature surface.

## What changed

- \`activation_registry.rs\` (3 tests): isActivated default, admin getter, unauthorized activate revert
- \`b20_precompile.rs\` (10 tests): factory create and transfer, metadata, approve/transferFrom, mint/burn, transferWithMemo, supply cap, metadata updates, pause/unpause, address prediction and isB20, duplicate salt revert
- \`policy_registry.rs\` (1 test): sentinel policyExists(0) = true
- \`policy_transfer.rs\` (8 tests): allowlist/blocklist gating, always-block, non-admin mutation reverts, batch membership atomicity, authorization persistence across blocks, default ALWAYS\_ALLOW before policy is wired
- \`devnet/src/b20.rs\`: \`B20PrecompileClient\` and \`B20CreateConfig\` RPC helpers shared across all B20 devnet tests

type=standard
risk=low
impact=sev5
commit_subjects: chore(BASE-000): update main branch to include some other changes
test(policy): add 5 unit tests to PolicyRegistry storage
feat(storage): introduce precompile-macros and precompile-storage crates
feat(precompiles): add base-common-precompiles crate and Beryl action tests
test(devnet): add B20 and PolicyRegistry E2E tests
fix(lychee): exclude pending-merge file links from link checker

items.author: ajsutton
items.message: chore: remove non-Go op-supervisor build and docs references (#20994)

key_prs.author: ajsutton

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Stacked on #20994.

Deletes op-supervisor down to the packages still imported from outside it (or transitively reachable from them):

- \`op-supervisor/supervisor/backend/cross\`
- \`op-supervisor/supervisor/backend/reads\`
- \`op-supervisor/supervisor/backend/superevents\`
- \`op-supervisor/supervisor/types\`
- \`op-supervisor/supervisor/backend/depset\` — still imported by op-chain-ops/interopgen, op-devstack/sysgo, and op-program; relocated separately in #20993.

Everything else (\`cmd\`, \`flags\`, \`config\`, \`metrics\`, \`Makefile\`, \`justfile\`, \`README\`, \`supervisor/frontend\`, \`supervisor/service.go\` / \`entrypoint.go\`, \`backend/{backend.go, db, processors, syncnode, l1access, rewinder, status, executor.go, mock.go, chain_metrics.go, config.go}\`) is supervisor-runtime code that no consumer needs anymore.

74 files / 18,049 deletions. \`go build ./...\`, \`go vet ./...\`, \`just lint-go\`, and \`go test ./op-supervisor/...\` all green.
commit_subjects: chore(op-supervisor): delete everything except the packages still used outside

key_prs.title: chore(op-supervisor): delete everything except packages still imported externally

key_prs.author: ajsutton

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Relocates `op-supervisor/supervisor/backend/depset` to `op-chain-ops/interopgen/config` and renames the Go package from `depset` to `config`. The package is a set of config value types (`FullConfigSet`, `StaticRollupConfigSet`, `DependencySet`, `StaticConfigDependency`, `LinkChecker`, …) — its non-supervisor consumers are `op-chain-ops/interopgen`, `op-devstack/sysgo`, and `op-program`.

Step toward deleting op-supervisor: removes one of the remaining reasons it has to exist as a Go module dependency. No logic changes — pure relocation, with import paths and qualifiers rewritten across both external consumers and op-supervisor's own files. Files that also import `op-supervisor/config` use the alias `interopcfg` to avoid the package-name collision.

Also adds `op-chain-ops/interopgen/config/` to op-program's `Dockerfile.vmcompat.dockerignore` and `Dockerfile.repro.dockerignore` so the vm-compat analysis can still build `op-program/client` after the move. The package's transitive deps stay inside the existing allow-list (op-service/op-core/op-node/op-alt-da).
commit_subjects: chore(interopgen): move depset package out of op-supervisor
fix: goimports grouping and op-program dockerignore

key_prs.title: chore(interopgen): move depset package to op-chain-ops/interopgen/config

content_signals.body_excerpt:
The `op-node interop dev-setup` and `op-node interop devkey` subcommands aren't invoked anywhere in the repo — no script, Makefile/justfile target, kurtosis package, devstack, e2e test, or CI workflow uses them. The e2e harness calls `interopgen.Deploy` in-process rather than shelling out.

Removing shrinks the op-node CLI surface and trims one consumer of `op-chain-ops/interopgen` on the path to deleting op-supervisor.
commit_subjects: chore(op-node): remove unused interop subcommand

key_prs.author: ajsutton

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Drops op-supervisor from the build/publish surfaces and architecture docs that describe it as a live component:

Build / publish:
- `justfile` — `TEST_PKGS` and the docker bake build target list
- `docker-bake.hcl` — `OP_SUPERVISOR_VERSION` variable and `op-supervisor` target
- `ops/docker/op-stack-go/Dockerfile` — `op-supervisor-builder` and `op-supervisor-target` stages
- `.github/images.json` — image publish entry

Docs:
- `AGENTS.md` — replace the op-supervisor entry with op-supernode, with a description that reflects what op-supernode actually does (multi-chain CL host with in-process cross-chain safety verification)
- `op-node/README.md` — drop the entire `Interoperability` subsection
- `op-devstack/README.md` — drop the `Supervisor` component line

Left in place: the top-level `README.md` directory listing, the Go package and docs inside `op-supervisor/`, the public-docs redirects in `docs.json`, the `opgeth-decoupling.md` deprecation note, and the dockerignore allowlists (still needed while op-program imports `op-supervisor/.../cross`).
commit_subjects: chore: remove non-Go op-supervisor build and docs references

key_prs.title: chore: remove non-Go op-supervisor build and architecture-doc references

content_signals.body_excerpt:
The dependency set is a global property shared by every chain a supernode manages, but it was previously only reachable via the per-VN `--interop.dependency-set` flag inherited from op-node. Add a supernode-level `--dependency-set` flag (env `OP_SUPERNODE_DEPENDENCY_SET`), load it once, and override each virtual node config's `DependencySet`. The per-VN flag is added to `SupernodeOwnedFlags` so setting it logs a warning.

Closes ethereum-optimism/protocol-team#96
commit_subjects: feat(op-supernode): add top-level --dependency-set flag

key_prs.author: pauldowman

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Adds a `kona-publish-prestates-on-tag` workflow that runs on `kona-client/v*` tag pushes and uploads hash-named kona prestates to GCS, mirroring the existing op-program tag-publish flow.

- Reuses the existing `kona-publish-prestate-artifacts` matrix job — no script changes; the existing upload code already takes the hash-named path when `<< pipeline.git.branch >>` is empty.
- Workflow is gated by `c-run_release` (already set on every tag push) with a job-level tag filter `^kona-client\/v.*` and `branches: ignore: /.*/` so it no-ops on PR/develop/merge-queue pushes.

**Testing**: since tags can't be deleted I can't easily test by creating a throwaway tag on this branch. I recommend just testing it with the next kona-client tag. If it fails we can still manually upload the prestate.
commit_subjects: ci(rust): publish kona prestates on kona-client/v* tag push

key_prs.title: ci(rust): publish kona prestates on kona-client/v* tag push

content_signals.body_excerpt:
The `op-node interop dev-setup` and `op-node interop devkey` subcommands aren't invoked anywhere in the repo — no script, Makefile/justfile target, kurtosis package, devstack, e2e test, or CI workflow uses them. The e2e harness calls `interopgen.Deploy` in-process rather than shelling out.

Removing shrinks the op-node CLI surface and trims one consumer of `op-chain-ops/interopgen` on the path to deleting op-supervisor.
commit_subjects: chore(op-node): remove unused interop subcommand

content_signals.body_excerpt:
The dependency set is a global property shared by every chain a supernode manages, but it was previously only reachable via the per-VN `--interop.dependency-set` flag inherited from op-node. Add a supernode-level `--dependency-set` flag (env `OP_SUPERNODE_DEPENDENCY_SET`), load it once, and override each virtual node config's `DependencySet`. The per-VN flag is added to `SupernodeOwnedFlags` so setting it logs a warning.

Closes ethereum-optimism/protocol-team#96
commit_subjects: feat(op-supernode): add top-level --dependency-set flag

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Drops op-supervisor from the build/publish surfaces and architecture docs that describe it as a live component:

Build / publish:
- `justfile` — `TEST_PKGS` and the docker bake build target list
- `docker-bake.hcl` — `OP_SUPERVISOR_VERSION` variable and `op-supervisor` target
- `ops/docker/op-stack-go/Dockerfile` — `op-supervisor-builder` and `op-supervisor-target` stages
- `.github/images.json` — image publish entry

Docs:
- `AGENTS.md` — replace the op-supervisor entry with op-supernode, with a description that reflects what op-supernode actually does (multi-chain CL host with in-process cross-chain safety verification)
- `op-node/README.md` — drop the entire `Interoperability` subsection
- `op-devstack/README.md` — drop the `Supervisor` component line

Left in place: the top-level `README.md` directory listing, the Go package and docs inside `op-supervisor/`, the public-docs redirects in `docs.json`, the `opgeth-decoupling.md` deprecation note, and the dockerignore allowlists (still needed while op-program imports `op-supervisor/.../cross`).
commit_subjects: chore: remove non-Go op-supervisor build and docs references

merged.title: chore: remove non-Go op-supervisor build and architecture-doc references

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Adds a `kona-publish-prestates-on-tag` workflow that runs on `kona-client/v*` tag pushes and uploads hash-named kona prestates to GCS, mirroring the existing op-program tag-publish flow.

- Reuses the existing `kona-publish-prestate-artifacts` matrix job — no script changes; the existing upload code already takes the hash-named path when `<< pipeline.git.branch >>` is empty.
- Workflow is gated by `c-run_release` (already set on every tag push) with a job-level tag filter `^kona-client\/v.*` and `branches: ignore: /.*/` so it no-ops on PR/develop/merge-queue pushes.

**Testing**: since tags can't be deleted I can't easily test by creating a throwaway tag on this branch. I recommend just testing it with the next kona-client tag. If it fails we can still manually upload the prestate.
commit_subjects: ci(rust): publish kona prestates on kona-client/v* tag push

opened.title: ci(rust): publish kona prestates on kona-client/v* tag push

releases.body:
## Overview

This is a patch release of op-proposer. The bulk of the changes are preparation for a future interop release; for non-interop deployments the main change is a bump of the underlying op-geth dependency. It is an optional upgrade for all users.

## What's Changed

### Interop preparation

- Fix: fail over to caught-up supernode sync status (#20615)
- Remove op-supervisor proposal source (#20779)
- Supernode: redefine `superroot_atTimestamp` `CurrentL1` as the block being pro
releases.name: op-proposer/v1.16.3
